idea-plugin.name: Pokemon
idea-plugin.description:
Adorable walking pixel art Pokemon pets that live in a tool window while you code. This is a JetBrains port of the popular vscode-pokemon extension by Jakob Hoeg Mørk, and it reuses the original animation engine and sprites, so the Pokémon behave exactly like they do in VS Code.

Features

  • Spawn any of 565 Pokémon from generations 1–4, searchable by name.
  • Random spawn with authentic 1-in-8192 shiny odds — shiny variants included ✨
  • Pokémon walk around, take naps, and befriend each other ❤️
  • Four sprite sizes: nano, small, medium, large.
  • Themed backgrounds: forest, castle, beach (light & dark variants follow your IDE theme).
  • Your Pokémon roster is remembered across IDE restarts.

Usage

Open the Pokemon tool window (right sidebar). Use the title-bar buttons to spawn a chosen Pokémon (+), spawn a random one (⚡), or remove one (−). The gear menu (⚙) has Pokémon size, background theme, roll call, and remove-all.

Works in PyCharm, DataSpell, IntelliJ IDEA, WebStorm, and every other IntelliJ-Platform IDE (2024.2+).

Credits: all Pokémon logic, sprites and assets come from the MIT-licensed jakobhoeg/vscode-pokemon project (also on the VS Code Marketplace). Pokémon sprites are property of Nintendo/Game Freak; this is a non-commercial fan project.
